message:
fix(search): correct dimension indexing in hash-based embeddings

Fixes an off-by-one error in the FNV-1a hash-based embedding generation
that could cause dimension index overflow and incorrect sparse vector
placement, leading to degraded similarity search quality.

Changes to src/search/embeddings.rs:

Bug description:
- Hash value was being used directly as dimension index
- Could exceed EMBEDDING_DIMS (384) causing silent wraparound
- Affected similarity calculations between skills
- More pronounced with certain text patterns

Fix:
- Apply modulo operation immediately after hash computation
- Ensure index is always within [0, EMBEDDING_DIMS)
- Add bounds checking assertion in debug builds
- Document the constraint in code comments

Impact on similarity search:
- Before: Some dimension collisions due to wraparound
- After: Proper distribution across all 384 dimensions
- Existing embeddings remain compatible (same algorithm)
- Slight improvement in search precision expected

The FNV-1a embedding approach:
- Deterministic: Same text always produces same embedding
- Fast: No external dependencies or ML models
- Sparse: Most dimensions are zero (memory efficient)
- Adequate: Good enough for skill similarity matching

This fix ensures the sparse dimensions are correctly populated,
improving the quality of nearest-neighbor searches used by
the suggestion system and duplicate detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
